title: Agoseris barbellulata
intro: Agoseris barbellulata, also known as the woolly marigold or woolly dandelion, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the western United States, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and woolly foliage. The plant is used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Agoseris barbellulata include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Agoseris barbellulata is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or infused oil, and is often used in traditional medicine to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Agoseris barbellulata is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family, Asteraceae. It is native to western North America and is commonly known as the short-rayed desert dandelion. The plant is classified within the genus Agoseris, which contains several other species of dandelion-like plants. Agoseris barbellulata is further classified as a member of the tribe Madinae, a group of plants that are primarily found in the deserts and dry regions of North America. This classification helps to identify the plant's unique characteristics and distinguish it from other related species.
uses: Agoseris barbellulata, also known as forked prickly phacelia or forked prickly lettuce, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves and roots have been employed to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties are believed to aid in wound healing and reducing pain. Additionally, Agoseris barbellulata has been used as a natural remedy for gastrointestinal problems and to promote digestive health. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Agoseris barbellulata as a medicinal agent.
